channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇰🇼 Iranian drone strike kills one at Kuwait International Airport
item.description: Iran struck Kuwait's main airport with drones, killing one person and injuring dozens, marking a major escalation as the US-Iran ceasefire is tested. The attack damaged the airport terminal and disrupted flights across the Gulf region.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇳🇿 China bans four New Zealand MPs over Taiwan visit
item.description: China imposed one-year entry bans on four New Zealand lawmakers after they visited Taiwan, in what Wellington called an "intimidation tactic." New Zealand's Foreign Minister Winston Peters demanded an explanation from Beijing.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇱 Trump acknowledges calling Netanyahu "crazy" amid Iran ceasefire tensions
item.description: President Trump confirmed he called Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu "crazy" and said Israel is complicating peace talks with Iran, in a rare public rift between the US and Israeli leaders.

item.title: 🇸🇴 Gunfire erupts in Mogadishu ahead of anti-government protests
item.description: Gunfire broke out in Somalia's capital before planned anti-government protests against President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud. Former PM Mohamed Khaire accused government forces of attacking him.

item.title: 🇬🇷🇺🇦 Greece Issues Formal Protest to Ukraine Over Naval Drone Found in Greek Waters
item.description: Greece has formally protested to Ukraine after discovering an explosives-laden naval drone in its waters last month. The incident adds diplomatic strain as Ukraine faces scrutiny over maritime operations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇺🇦 US House Advances Ukraine Aid Bill Despite Trump Administration Opposition
item.description: The US House of Representatives has advanced the Ukraine Support Act, setting up a final vote on June 4. The bill moves forward despite opposition from the Trump administration and Republican leadership.

item.title: 🇭🇺🕊️ Hungary Offers to Host Ukraine-Russia Peace Talks
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar has offered Budapest as a venue for Ukraine-Russia negotiations. He emphasized Hungary can provide diplomatic and humanitarian aid but cannot play a decisive role.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇪🇺 NATO Chief Rutte Arrives in Kyiv After Mass Russian Strike Kills Over 20
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte visited Kyiv one day after one of Russia's heaviest attacks killed over 20 people and injured more than 100 across Ukraine. The visit underscores continued Western support.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱 Israel and Lebanon agree to implement ceasefire if Hezbollah halts attacks
item.description: US-mediated deal requires Hezbollah to cease strikes; both nations reject any actor holding Lebanon hostage. State Department announces agreement as regional tensions persist.

item.title: 🇰🇼🇮🇷⚔️ Iranian drone strike on Kuwait airport kills one, wounds dozens
item.description: Iran claims retaliation for earlier US strikes on its oil facilities. Attack tests regional ceasefire agreements as tensions escalate in Persian Gulf.

item.title: 🇨🇳 MI5 warns Chinese spies using job websites to target government workers
item.description: Five Eyes intelligence alliance warns that Chinese agents are using LinkedIn, Indeed and Upwork to post fake analyst jobs to extract state secrets from UK and Western government employees.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Former MI6 chief Alex Younger dies aged 62 after cancer battle
item.description: Sir Alex Younger led Britains Secret Intelligence Service from 2014 to 2020, serving as its longest-serving chief in 50 years. He played key roles in counter-terrorism work before and after 9/11.

item.title: 🇧🇩 Bangladeshs Khalilur Rahman elected UN General Assembly president in tight race
item.description: Bangladesh foreign minister defeated Cyprus ambassador 99-91 in secret ballot. He will lead UNGA during selection of next UN Secretary-General, taking helm September 8.

item.title: 🇨🇳⚓ Chinese sailless submarine caught on satellite imagery at Shanghai shipyard
item.description: A new type of Chinese submarine that appears to lack a traditional sail has emerged at Jiangnan Shipyard in Shanghai, potentially representing a new class designed for high-speed underwater operations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump privately told aides he would end Iran ceasefire if US troops killed
item.description: US President Donald Trump has told aides privately that he would consider ending the truce deal with Iran if it killed American troops, the Wall Street Journal reported citing US officials.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇸🇾 Israeli forces advance into southwestern Syria, shell areas near Daraa
item.description: Israeli forces shelled areas in southwestern Syria and advanced into the countryside of Daraa province, with three Israeli vehicles pushing into Wadi al-Raqad Road in the Yarmouk Basin area before withdrawing.

item.title: 🦠 DRC Ebola outbreak may have started as early as January, WHO says
item.description: The WHO chief warned that the virus "had a big head start" and that response efforts are being hindered by travel restrictions and community mistrust.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Strikes Kill Nine in Lebanon as Hezbollah Fires Rockets
item.description: Israeli strikes killed nine people in Lebanon as Hezbollah fired rockets over the border, straining a partial ceasefire agreed between Israel and the Lebanese armed group.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Ukraine-Hungary Minority Rights Agreement Clears Path for EU Accession
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar announced an agreement with Ukraine over the rights of the Hungarian-speaking minority, removing a key obstacle to Ukraine's EU accession negotiations.

item.title: ⚖️ Haredi Protesters Storm Home of Israeli Supreme Court Judge Over IDF Draft
item.description: Ultra-Orthodox demonstrators breached the home of a senior Israeli judge amid protests against arrests of yeshiva students who refuse military service, highlighting deep social divisions in Israel.

item.title: 🕵️ US and Five Eyes Allies Warn of Chinese Espionage via LinkedIn Recruitment
item.description: The FBI, MI5 and other Western intelligence agencies issued a joint warning that Chinese spies are using professional networking sites like LinkedIn to recruit people with access to classified information.

item.title: 🏛️ Portugal and Austria Defeat Germany for UN Security Council Seats
item.description: Portugal and Austria won seats on the UN Security Council, defeating Germany in a vote. Kyrgyzstan also secured a seat for the first time, beating the Philippines 143-49.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US House Approves War Powers Resolution to Halt Iran Military Action
item.description: The House voted 215-208 to rein in presidential war powers on Iran, with four Republicans joining Democrats. The vote represents a significant congressional rebuke to Trump over the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🛢️ Russian Oil Pipeline to Hungary and Slovakia Resumes After Months-Long Outage
item.description: Russian oil exports to Hungary and Slovakia via the Druzhba pipeline have returned to normal after a months-long outage that had fueled tensions between Ukraine and the Central European countries.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇱💬 Trump confirms calling Netanyahu "crazy" as Israel complicates Iran peace talks
item.description: President Trump acknowledged he called Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu "crazy" and accused Israel of complicating peace negotiations with Iran. The comments come amid ongoing ceasefire discussions following Iranian strikes.
